sections.title: 1. Africa's Political Regions
points: Africa is divided into six major geographical and political regions.
A political region is a group of countries located in the same area that often share similar history, climate, or economic goals.
Regional organizations like EAC, ECOWAS, and SADC help these countries trade and work together.

sections.title: 2. East Africa (The EAC)
points: Members: Uganda, Kenya, Tanzania, Rwanda, Burundi, South Sudan, DR Congo, and Somalia.
Regional Body: East African Community (EAC).
Key Capitals: Kampala (Uganda), Nairobi (Kenya), Dodoma (Tanzania), Kigali (Rwanda), Gitega (Burundi), Juba (South Sudan).

sections.title: 3. West Africa (ECOWAS)
points: This is the most populous region in Africa, largely due to Nigeria.
Regional Body: Economic Community of West African States (ECOWAS).
Key Capitals: Abuja (Nigeria), Accra (Ghana), Dakar (Senegal), Bamako (Mali), Ouagadougou (Burkina Faso), Freetown (Sierra Leone).

sections.title: 4. North Africa and the Horn
points: North Africa: Borders the Mediterranean. Algeria (Largest country) is here. Capitals: Cairo (Egypt), Tripoli (Libya), Rabat (Morocco), Algiers (Algeria).
The Horn of Africa: Named for its rhino-horn shape. Capitals: Addis Ababa (Ethiopia), Mogadishu (Somalia), Asmara (Eritrea), Djibouti City (Djibouti).
Note: Addis Ababa is the headquarters of the African Union (AU).

sections.title: 5. Central and Southern Africa
points: Central Africa: The heart of Africa, dominated by rainforests. Capitals: Kinshasa (DRC), Bangui (CAR), Libreville (Gabon), Yaounde (Cameroon).
Southern Africa: Mineral-rich tip of the continent. Regional Body: SADC. Capitals: Pretoria (South Africa), Lusaka (Zambia), Harare (Zimbabwe), Windhoek (Namibia).

sections.title: 6. Africa's Island Capitals
points: Madagascar: Antananarivo
Seychelles: Victoria
Mauritius: Port Louis
Comoros: Moroni
Cape Verde: Praia
Sao Tome & Principe: Sao Tome

sections.text: PLE Fact: Ethiopia is the only African country that was never colonized by Europeans. Its capital, Addis Ababa, is often called the 'Diplomatic Capital of Africa'.

cards.fact: The capital of Ethiopia and headquarters of the African Union (AU).
A country completely surrounded by the territory of another country (e.g., Lesotho in South Africa).
The two closest capital cities in the world, separated only by the Congo River.
cards.mnemonic: The Twin Capitals of the Congo.

cards.fact: The regional economic organization for West African states.
